statement: A reusable GOVERNMENT AI-coding governance baseline already exists: the UK GDS/DSIT 'AI Coding Assistants for developers in HMG' (Sept 2025) sets ten reusable principles overlapping agent-enterprise's build-time defense and review controls.

evidence.quote: Merges made to the main branch need to be subject to human peer review ... secrets are separated from AICAs ... multi-layered approach to vulnerability scanning ... You should only commit code changes that you understand

falsifier: If the GOV.UK document were withdrawn or shown to be discussion-only with no reusable principles, the redundancy claim would weaken.

control_anchor.framework: UK GDS AI Coding Assistants Guidance
control_anchor.control_id: Ten principles (branch protection, secrets, scanning, review)
